conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, your son Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, **how old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know that Alex is 20 years old.

Since Alex is 20, this is an important factor. Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, but can continue until they're under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (This includes things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or home education if it started before they turned 16.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the rules, Child Benefit normally applies to children under 16. For children aged 16-19, you can still qualify if they remain in approved education or training. However, since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Summary:**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Reason:** Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, and can continue until age 20 only if the young person remains in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 and not in such education or training, he doesn't meet the age criteria for Child Benefit.

If Alex were to start approved education or training before turning 20, you could potentially claim Child Benefit again during that period. If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 and not in approved education or training, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit eligibility extends only to children under 16, or up to age 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex exceeds the age limit without being in qualifying education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
